segments.text: And David fled from Naioth in Ramah, and came and said before Jonathan,
What have I done? what is mine iniquity? and what is my sin before thy father, that he seeketh my life?
segments.speaker: David

segments.text: God forbid; thou shalt not die: behold, my father will do nothing either great or small, but that he will shew it me: and why should my father hide this thing from me? it is not so.
segments.speaker: Jonathan

segments.text: Thy father certainly knoweth that I have found grace in thine eyes; and he saith, Let not Jonathan know this, lest he be grieved: but truly as the LORD liveth, and as thy soul liveth, there is but a step between me and death.
segments.speaker: David

segments.text: Whatsoever thy soul desireth, I will even do it for thee.
segments.speaker: Jonathan

footnotes: Whatsoever…: or, Say what is thy mind and I will do, etc

segments.text: Behold, to morrow is the new moon, and I should not fail to sit with the king at meat: but let me go, that I may hide myself in the field unto the third day at even.
segments.speaker: David

segments.text: Come, and let us go out into the field.
segments.speaker: Jonathan

segments.text: And they went out both of them into the field.
O LORD God of Israel, when I have sounded my father about to morrow any time, or the third day, and, behold, if there be good toward David, and I then send not unto thee, and shew it thee;
segments.speaker: Jonathan

segments.text: So David hid himself in the field: and when the new moon was come, the king sat him down to eat meat.

heading: David Absent from the King's Table

segments.text: And as soon as the lad was gone, David arose out of a place toward the south, and fell on his face to the ground, and bowed himself three times: and they kissed one another, and wept one with another, until David exceeded.
Go in peace, forasmuch as we have sworn both of us in the name of the LORD, saying, The LORD be between me and thee, and between my seed and thy seed for ever.
segments.speaker: Jonathan

segments.text: And he arose and departed: and Jonathan went into the city.

footnotes: forasmuch…: or, the LORD be witness of that which etc
